chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No. S- 653 of 2021 Syed Nazeer-ul-Hassan ------------------- Petitioner VERSUS SP Complaint Cell Hyderabad & others ----------------- Respondents Dates of hearing: 22.08.2022 Date of decision: 22.08.2022 None present for Petitioner Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Addl.A.G. O R D E R ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON, J-. Through instant petition, the petitioner has prayed for direction to respondents 1 and 2 to provide legal protection and also to direct private respondents not to harass him without due course of law. 2. Case the petitioner is that he is retired banker and he filed one suit against the Committee of Noorul Islam Jamia Masjid which is pending before 5th Senior Civil Judge, Hyderabad; that the petitioner is running a shop in the said Masjid on rent and due to above suit committee of the Masjid has lodged two FIRs against him; that petitioner also filed application under Section 22-A & B Cr.P.C. for protection against private respondents which was allowed but yet they are causing harassment and issuing threats of implication in false cases, hence the instant petition. 3. Primarily, the dispute has been alleged between private parties concerning obligations arising out of contracts or breach of legal or moral duties; and in this background, it has been alleged that the private parties at fault are harassing the petitioners either themselves and/or with the help of police officials and instead of taking action against the wrongdoers, the police is supporting them. 4. Petitioner alleging harassment against police officials, at this stage, learned AAG has submitted that police shall ensure no harassment is caused to the petitioner, learned counsel is satisfied with the statement of learned AAG and seeks disposal of the instant petition in terms thereof. 5.

Primarily, the dispute has been alleged between private parties concerning obligations arising out of contracts or breach of legal or moral duties; and in this background, it has been alleged that the private parties at fault are harassing the petitioners either themselves and/or with the help of police officials and instead of taking action against the wrongdoers, the police is supporting them. 4. Petitioner alleging harassment against police officials, at this stage, learned AAG has submitted that police shall ensure no harassment is caused to the petitioner, learned counsel is satisfied with the statement of learned AAG and seeks disposal of the instant petition in terms thereof. 5. In view of the above by consent this petition is disposed of with the understanding that police officers shall be neutral if there arises any dispute between the private parties and shall act under the law. JUDGE Karar_hussain/PS*
